independent_codex_audit.scope: Independent repo/code validation plus small local import smoke checks; no long training, no huge model/checkpoint downloads, no staging or commits.
independent_codex_audit.recommended_next_action: Email the informed-correctors authors for the Text8 HollowMD4 checkpoint, exact paper training config, vocabulary file, and sampling/evaluation commands before training from scratch.

bottom_line.informed_correctors_text8: Scientifically principled second backend, but no public weights found and engineering/training cost should be benchmarked before a full run.
bottom_line.prism_llada: Not recommended as a timing-validation backend because it is a token-quality/remasking fine-tuning system with no public PRISM LLaDA checkpoint found.

claims.claim: No informed-correctors Text8 weights are available.
claims.evidence: No checkpoint-like files found locally; README and grep/web search did not reveal public Text8 weights.
Local config uses 1M steps, batch 512, seq_len 256, 1000 timesteps, 36 layers, 12 heads, hidden_dim 2048. Runtime requires a measured benchmark.
Architecture structurally excludes x_d from the output at coordinate d; logits are learned model conditionals/proxies, not exact true data conditionals.
fori_loop blocks Python schedule membership; a boolean schedule array with jax.lax.cond inside the compiled loop is cleaner than a Python loop for large sweeps.

claims.claim: Timing control is approximately one day engineering.
claims.evidence: Current code uses static self.k, ignores sample_step k/temp args, and does not wire config.k. Robust scheduled sampling with CRN tests is estimated at 2-4 days.
PRISM_llada/generate_samples.py defaults to /n/netscratch/sham_lab/Everyone/jay_llada/llada_PRISM/checkpoint-44000; no local PRISM checkpoint files found.

claims.claim: PRISM remasking head is entangled with training objective.
claims.evidence: RemaskingTrainer.compute_loss trains remasking_conf from one-step sampled xs correctness labels plus unmasking loss.

claims.claim: PRISM timing cannot be separated cleanly from token selection.
claims.evidence: Inference remasks top-k tokens selected by learned quality/confidence; changing times changes a learned token-quality correction loop, not only a fixed corrector schedule.

informed_correctors.license_audit: No root LICENSE file found; many source files contain Apache-2.0 headers inherited from DeepMind code.
informed_correctors.checkpoint_format: Orbax CheckpointManager saving train_state and Grain train_iter under <workdir>/checkpoints.
main_risks: Text8 data path is hardcoded to /root/md4/data_dir/text8/text8.zip.
hollow_md4 Text8 config omits vocab_dir even though train.py opens config.vocab_dir.
Timing schedule requires model/sampler API edits, not just a for-loop swap.
informed_correctors.terminal_F_primary: Negative native Text8 invalid-word or invalid-character rate using a fixed training vocabulary.
informed_correctors.terminal_F_backup: Model-based bits-per-token or ELBO-style self-score under the trained diffusion model.

prism_llada.repo_state: Local worktree is behind remote main; remote files were inspected via git fetch/git show without checkout.
main_risks: No public PRISM LLaDA checkpoint found.
Training/fine-tuning is an 8B LoRA project, not a small validation gate.
Remasking confidence is trained as token-quality machinery.
Timing-only diagnostics would be confounded by token selection and code pass@k noise.
Local environment lacks datasets, peft, evaluate, and wandb.

candidates.name: informed-correctors / Text8 / HollowMD4
candidates.logit_note: p(x_d | x_{-d}, t) by structural masking — forward/backward stream padding excludes x_d from attending to itself
candidates.timing_control_engineering: 1 day — replace jax.lax.fori_loop with Python for-loop, pass do_correct[i] per step
candidates.dataset: Text8 (char-level, 27 tokens, 100M chars, auto-download via tfds)
candidates.smoke_test_hpc_prognosis: workable — jax[cuda] wheel exists, no nvcc needed
blocking_issues: No pretrained checkpoint — must train 1M steps
JAX/Flax not installed in project venv (installable on HPC)
candidates.next_action: Install JAX env on HPC remdm311, write Python-loop sampler wrapper, submit training sbatch after Codex review

candidates.name: PRISM LLaDA
candidates.logit_note: RemaskingHead is a 2-layer MLP trained jointly with correction objective — not a separable quality signal
candidates.timing_control_engineering: requires new training objective — not engineering, a new research contribution
candidates.smoke_test_hpc_prognosis: high risk — peft missing, 16GB download, cuda-nvcc in requirements.yaml (CLAUDE.md rule #4 violation)
blocking_issues: Timing entangled with remasking_conf training objective — not separable without retraining
requirements.yaml specifies cuda-nvcc (triggers HPC sbatch crash)

verdict.rejection_reason: Timing entangled at training; remasking_conf head trained jointly with correction objective; not separable without new training objective
verdict.recommendation_reason: Hollow transformer provides structural guarantee p(x_d | x_{-d}, t) — directly aligned with corrector Gibbs-sampling mechanism; timing toggle is 1-day engineering fix; small char-level model enables fast K=30 experiments once checkpoint exists
prerequisite_gates: G0-TEXT8: JAX+flax import smoke + T=4 CPU run on slnode
T8-H: K=10 B=2 T=64 headroom gate after training

current_operational_plan.recommendation: pursue informed-correctors/Text8; do not pursue PRISM LLaDA for timing validation unless conditions change
